title: Ground Turkey Egg Roll in a Bowl (High-Protein Recipe) | Eati
introduction: Ground Turkey Egg Roll in a Bowl gives you egg-roll flavors without the wrapper. It is high-protein, fast, and easy to portion, making it a great meal prep option for your Eati logging.

sections.heading: Ingredients
sections.content:
- Ground turkey
- Cabbage
- Carrots
- Soy sauce
- Ginger
- Garlic
- Sesame oil (optional)
- Salt and pepper

sections.heading: Instructions
sections.content:
1. Brown ground turkey in a pan with garlic and a pinch of salt.
2. Add ginger and stir until fragrant.
3. Saute cabbage and carrots until slightly tender.
4. Add soy sauce and combine; cook until flavors blend.
5. Finish with optional sesame oil, serve warm, and log your portion in Eati.

sections.heading: Calories, Fat, and Protein (Estimated)
sections.content:
Calories: 243 kcal per serving (estimated)
Fat: 8.2g
Protein: 30g

Tip: reduce oil to keep fat lower while keeping flavor through seasonings.

sections.heading: Why Egg Roll in a Bowl Beats Actual Egg Rolls
sections.content:
Egg roll in a bowl (sometimes called 'crack slaw') is one of the most popular low-carb meal prep recipes of the last five years. Here's why it makes sense for weight loss.

Traditional egg rolls (restaurant, 1 fried):
• 200–230 calories
• 5g protein
• 10g fat (mostly from frying)
• 20g carbs (from wrapper)
• Greasy, low satiety

This recipe (1 serving = large bowl):
• 243 calories
• 30g protein
• 8g fat
• 12g carbs (from vegetables)
• Hearty, very filling

You're getting 6x the protein at similar calories — without the fried wrapper.

Key comparisons:
• PF Chang's Veggie Egg Roll: 280 cal, 5g protein each.
• Trader Joe's Chicken Egg Rolls: 260 cal, 11g protein per 2 rolls.
• Panda Express Chow Mein (side): 510 cal, 13g protein.
• This recipe: 243 cal, 30g protein per bowl.

Why ground turkey?
• Leaner than ground beef (93% lean vs 80%).
• Neutral flavor takes seasonings well.
• Cooks in 8 minutes.
• About $5–7/lb (affordable).

Can you use 99% lean ground turkey?
Yes, but it can get dry. Mix with 93/7 for best flavor, or add 1 tsp sesame oil to prevent dryness.

Protein breakdown (4 oz cooked ground turkey):
• 93/7 lean: 170 cal, 24g protein, 8g fat.
• 99% lean: 120 cal, 26g protein, 2g fat.
• 85/15 lean: 220 cal, 22g protein, 14g fat.

Use a calorie calculator to plan your week.

sections.heading: The Cabbage Advantage: A Secret Weight Loss Vegetable
sections.content:
Cabbage is criminally underrated as a weight-loss food. Here's why it makes this recipe so filling.

Cabbage nutrition (1 cup shredded):
• 22 calories
• 2g fiber
• 37% DV vitamin C
• 56% DV vitamin K
• 2g sugar, virtually no fat
• Very low glycemic index

Why cabbage fills you up:
• High water content (92% water).
• High fiber-to-calorie ratio.
• Slow to eat (has to be chewed thoroughly).
• Large volume per calorie — a 2-cup serving = 44 cal.

Types of cabbage for this recipe:

Green cabbage:
• Most common, budget-friendly.
• Firm texture holds up to stir-frying.
• Mild, slightly sweet flavor.

Savoy cabbage:
• Crinkly leaves with tender texture.
• Softer, more delicate.
• Best for 'restaurant-style' feel.

Napa (Chinese) cabbage:
• Most authentic for Asian-inspired dishes.
• Tender, mild, slight crunch.
• Cooks faster than green cabbage.

Red/purple cabbage:
• Antioxidant-rich (anthocyanins).
• Slightly tougher texture.
• Adds color.

Coleslaw mix (time-saver):
• Pre-shredded bagged mix with green, red, and carrots.
• Fastest option.
• Slight texture loss but acceptable.

Pro tip: 'Shredded cabbage cooked' volume shrinks about 50% during cooking. Start with 4 cups raw for a generous 2 cups cooked.

Other low-calorie volume vegetables to add:
• Bell peppers (30 cal per cup)
• Mushrooms (15 cal per cup)
• Bean sprouts (30 cal per cup)
• Celery (20 cal per cup)
• Zucchini (20 cal per cup)
• Snow peas (30 cal per cup)

For more low-calorie veggies to incorporate, see [high-protein low-calorie foods](/blog/high-protein-low-calorie-foods) and [best foods for weight loss](/blog/best-foods-for-weight-loss).

sections.heading: Authentic Flavor Building: Seasoning Like a Restaurant
sections.content:
The difference between 'meh' egg roll bowl and restaurant-quality is in the seasoning.

The classic flavor stack:

Umami foundations:
• 3 tbsp soy sauce (or coconut aminos for paleo)
• 1 tbsp oyster sauce (adds depth, not very fishy)
• 1 tsp fish sauce (optional but transformative)
• 1 tsp rice vinegar (brightens the fat)

Aromatics:
• 4 cloves garlic, minced
• 2 inches fresh ginger, grated
• 3 scallions, sliced (white parts cooked, green parts garnish)

Finishing touches:
• 1 tsp toasted sesame oil (add at end — don't cook with it)
• 1 tbsp toasted sesame seeds
• 1–2 tsp sriracha or chili garlic sauce
• Fresh cilantro (optional)

Sauce variations:

1. Original (as written):
Soy + ginger + garlic + sesame oil.

2. Sweet and sour:
Add 1 tbsp rice vinegar + 1 tsp honey + pineapple chunks.

3. Spicy Sichuan:
Add 1 tbsp chili garlic sauce + 1 tsp Sichuan peppercorns + red pepper flakes.

4. Korean-inspired:
Add 1 tbsp gochujang + 1 tsp sesame oil + kimchi garnish.

5. Thai-inspired:
Add 2 tsp fish sauce + 1 tbsp peanut butter + 1 tsp lime + Thai basil.

6. Teriyaki-style:
Add 1 tbsp brown sugar + 1 tbsp mirin + 1 tbsp sake.

7. Buffalo fusion:
Add 2 tbsp buffalo sauce + blue cheese crumbles to top.

Chef-level techniques:
• Bloom aromatics first: Sauté garlic and ginger 30 seconds in dry pan before adding turkey. Releases oils and flavor compounds.
• Add soy sauce in two stages: Half at start for flavor penetration, half at end for fresh punch.
• Toast sesame seeds: Dry skillet, medium heat, 2 minutes until golden — 10x more flavorful than raw.
• Finish with fresh: Cilantro, scallion greens, crispy fried onions at serving time.

sections.heading: Serving Ideas: Bowls, Wraps, Stir-Fries
sections.content:
Egg roll in a bowl is endlessly adaptable for different macro goals and preferences.

Low-carb bowls (250–350 cal):
• As-is over cauliflower rice (35 cal per cup extra).
• Over shredded lettuce with rice wine vinaigrette.
• In lettuce cup wraps (Thai-style).
• Topped with fried egg (+70 cal, +6g protein).
• Stuffed into halved bell peppers.

Moderate-carb bowls (350–500 cal):
• Over 1/2 cup brown or jasmine rice.
• Stuffed into whole-grain tortilla as 'stir-fry wrap.'
• Over 1/2 cup rice noodles.
• With 1/4 cup edamame on top.

Higher-protein bowls:
• Top with scrambled egg or fried egg (+70 cal, +6g protein).
• Top with shredded rotisserie chicken (+30 cal per oz, +7g protein).
• Mix with cubed firm tofu (+80 cal, +10g protein per 1/2 cup).
• Top with leftover salmon flakes.

Kid-friendly adjustments:
• Reduce or omit ginger.
• Skip spicy sauces.
• Add small diced broccoli (kids love it small).
• Serve over white rice.
• Top with mild cheese (surprisingly good).

'Deconstructed egg roll' presentation:
• Serve on platter with crispy wonton strips for dipping (30 cal for 1 oz).
• Garnish with scallions and sesame seeds.
• Great for game days or gatherings.

Complete plate examples:

Fat loss (320 cal, 32g protein):
• 1 bowl egg roll in a bowl (243)
• 1 cup cauliflower rice (35)
• 1 cup snap peas (40)
• 1 tbsp sriracha (5)

Maintenance (450 cal, 38g protein):
• 1 bowl egg roll in a bowl (243)
• 1/2 cup brown rice (110)
• 1 fried egg (70)
• Cucumber salad (30)

Performance (600 cal, 42g protein):
• 1 bowl egg roll in a bowl + extra turkey (320)
• 3/4 cup rice (165)
• 2 fried eggs (140)

sections.heading: Meal Prep Strategy, Storage & Variations
sections.content:
Egg roll in a bowl is one of the best meal prep recipes — the flavors get better after 24 hours.

Best for meal prep because:
• Flavors deepen overnight.
• No delicate components to get soggy.
• Reheats evenly.
• Keeps 5+ days in the fridge.

Batch cooking:
• Double the recipe in a wok or 5-qt Dutch oven.
• Same cook time (12–15 minutes).
• Makes 8 servings.

Storage:
• Fridge: 5 days airtight.
• Freezer: 3 months (cabbage softens somewhat but still good).

Reheating:
• Skillet (best): Medium heat, 5 min. Add 1 tbsp water to steam.
• Microwave: 90 seconds covered. Stir halfway.
• From frozen: Thaw overnight then reheat; or microwave 4–5 min stirring.

Freezer meal prep approach:
• Cook full double batch Sunday.
• Portion into individual freezer-safe containers.
• Freeze immediately after cooling.
• Grab one from freezer before work — thaws at room temp by lunch.

Variations for variety:

Protein swaps:
• Ground chicken
• Ground pork (more flavor, more fat)
• Ground beef (93/7)
• Shrimp (add last 4 min)
• Firm tofu (crumbled, vegan option)

Vegetable variations:
• Add broccoli (+20 cal per cup)
• Add mushrooms (+15 cal per cup)
• Add bell peppers (+30 cal per cup)
• Add water chestnuts (+60 cal per 1/2 cup, great crunch)
• Add bamboo shoots (+15 cal per 1/2 cup)

Dietary adaptations:
• Paleo: Use coconut aminos instead of soy sauce.
• Keto: Already keto-friendly (under 10g net carbs).
• Gluten-free: Use tamari or certified gluten-free soy sauce.
• Whole30: Coconut aminos + compliant toppings.

Speed hacks:
• Pre-chopped coleslaw mix saves 5 minutes prep.
• Pre-minced garlic and ginger (jarred) saves 3 minutes.
• Total time with shortcuts: 15 minutes from start to plate.

For related recipes, see [high protein chicken taco soup](/blog/high-protein-chicken-taco-soup-recipe) and [30 best high-protein recipes](/blog/30-best-high-protein-recipes). For more low-calorie tips, see [how to track calories correctly](/blog/how-to-track-calories-correctly).

conclusion: This bowl is a convenient way to stay high-protein and consistent. Track it in Eati and adjust your portion size as needed.
metaTitle: Ground Turkey Egg Roll in a Bowl: 30g Protein Low-Carb Dinner (243 Cal)
metaDescription: Fast low-carb ground turkey egg roll in a bowl with 30g protein in 243 calories per serving. Includes cabbage guide, 7 sauce variations, and meal prep tips — ready in 15 minutes.
targetKeyword: ground turkey egg roll in a bowl high protein

faqs.question: How many calories in turkey egg roll in a bowl?
faqs.answer: Approximately 243 calories per serving with 30g of protein and 8g of fat. This is one of the leanest complete meals you can make — significantly lower than equivalent restaurant stir-fries (typically 500+ cal with half the protein).

faqs.question: Can I make egg roll in a bowl ahead of time?
faqs.answer: Yes — this recipe is actually better after 24 hours as flavors deepen. Store in the fridge for up to 5 days or freeze for 3 months. Reheat in a skillet with 1 tbsp water to keep it from drying out.

faqs.question: Is egg roll in a bowl keto?
faqs.answer: Yes. With under 10g net carbs per serving (entirely from vegetables), this fits keto macros. Use coconut aminos instead of soy sauce for a paleo/strictly low-carb version. Add more fat (avocado, sesame oil, fried egg) to hit keto ratios.

faqs.question: Can I use ground chicken or beef instead?
faqs.answer: Absolutely. Ground chicken has nearly identical macros (swap 1:1). Ground beef 93/7 adds ~40 calories per serving. Ground pork adds more flavor but also more fat (~80 extra calories per serving). Shrimp works too — add in the last 4 minutes.

faqs.question: What's the difference between egg roll in a bowl and crack slaw?
faqs.answer: They're essentially the same recipe with different names. 'Crack slaw' is a keto community term; 'egg roll in a bowl' is more common now. Some variations include eggs scrambled in; some use different proteins — core concept is identical: deconstructed egg roll filling with Asian seasonings over vegetables.
